title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common protocol. The video explains MCP architecture including client-server communication patterns, JSON-RPC messaging, local vs remote server configurations, and use cases with frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard for connecting AI agents to external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. It covers MCP architecture, including client-server communication patterns and JSON-RPC messaging, along with the distinction between local and remote server configurations. Use cases are demonstrated with LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop, showing how MCP standardizes the integration layer that agents rely on to reach outside resources.
